growth_records.medium: Anaerobic batch medium with lactate and glucose as main electron donors (composition not stated in main text)

up.method: reported qualitatively; preferentially consumes lactate over glucose and grows rapidly when doing so; lactate quantified by HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H, 5 mM H2SO4, 0.6 ml/min, 50C); numeric time-course only in Fig. 4A
reported qualitatively; consumed after lactate depletion; quantified by HPLC; numeric time-course only in Fig. 4A
reported qualitatively; acetate consumed as co-substrate of chain elongation (Fig. 4A)

sec.method: reported qualitatively; produced from lactate via the acrylate pathway (Fig. 4A, Fig. 6A)
reported qualitatively; C4 produced via reverse beta-oxidation/chain elongation, more active during glucose consumption (Fig. 4A)
reported qualitatively; C5-C8 VFA production dynamics shown (Fig. 4A bottom panels)

yields.ATP_yield_acrylate_pathway: 0.33 mol ATP per mol lactate (Enzyme Cost Minimization model prediction for the acrylate pathway, which M. elsdenii runs)

growth_records.cite: Strachan et al. Distinct lactate utilization strategies drive niche differentiation between two co-existing Megasphaera species in the rumen microbiome. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf147. [PMC12342382]
growth_records.snippet: the acrylate pathway yields 0.33 mole ATP per mole lactate, whereas rBOX yields 0.5. ... M. elsdenii should have a higher maximum possible growth rate on lactate at the expense of ATP yield ... M. elsdenii preferentially utilizes lactate vi
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic co-fed lactate+glucose experiment (Fig. 4A). No numeric maximum specific growth rate, doubling time, qS, or qP is given in the main-text XML: growth curves, substrate consumption and VFA (C2-C6,C8) production are shown only graphically in Fig. 4/Fig. 6 and max specific growth rate calculation is in Supplementary Materials & Methods / Supplementary Fig. S3B (not in this file). Temperature and pH not stated for the lactate+glucose experiment; a separate lactate-as-main-carbon-source experiment used a starting media pH of 5.5 (both species raised pH; effect larger for M. elsdenii). ATP-per-lactate yield 0.33 is an Enzyme Cost Minimization (ECM) model prediction (k+ assumed 200 1/s, Km 0.2 mM), NOT an experimentally measured biomass/product yield; recorded as stated. M. elsdenii inferred to have ~1.5-fold higher microbial population replication rate than M. hexanoica (Supplementary Table S2). No arithmetic possible from main text.
